member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Migration.MigrationExecutionPolicy
member.summary: Execution-time retry/abort policy applied when a migration plan runs. Pure contract — lives in the models assembly so both the engine's IMigrationManager and the Studio's migration facade can share it without the models project referencing the engine.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Migration.MigrationExecutionPolicy.AbortOnStepFailure
member.summary: When true (default), the plan aborts on the first non-transient step failure. When false, the plan continues to the next step on failure, recording the failed step in MigrationExecutionResult.FailedSteps. Dependency blocks and pre-flight gates always abort regardless of this flag.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.SchemaMigration.IMigrationProviderRegistry
member.summary:
Resolves an for a given data source using a 3-tier strategy:
Tier 1 — exact type override: a provider registered for the exact (typically discovered via SchemaMigrationProviderAttribute).
Tier 2 — category fallback: a provider registered as the default for the (e.g. RDBMS → SQL provider, Connector → read-only provider).
Tier 3 — null: the null provider, which reports every operation as unsupported.
never returns null.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.SchemaMigration.IMigrationProviderRegistry.Register(TheTechIdea.Beep.Utilities.DataSourceType,System.Func{TheTechIdea.Beep.IDataSource,TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.SchemaMigration.ISchemaMigrationProvider})
member.summary: Registers a Tier-1 factory for an exact . The factory receives the live being migrated.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.SchemaMigration.IMigrationProviderRegistry.RegisterCategoryFallback(TheTechIdea.Beep.Utilities.DatasourceCategory,System.Func{TheTechIdea.Beep.IDataSource,TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.SchemaMigration.ISchemaMigrationProvider})
member.summary: Registers a Tier-2 fallback factory for a . Used when no exact-type override is registered.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.SchemaMigration.IMigrationProviderRegistry.Resolve(TheTechIdea.Beep.IDataSource)
member.summary: Resolves a provider for the given data source. Never returns null. Order: exact type → category fallback → .

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.SchemaMigration.IMigrationProviderRegistry.RegisteredTypes
member.summary: Snapshot of every Tier-1 type currently registered (for diagnostics/tests).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.SchemaMigration.SchemaMigrationOp
member.summary: Logical schema operations a can perform. These are intentionally provider-agnostic — never SQL strings. Each provider translates an operation into its data source's native API.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.SchemaMigration.SchemaMigrationCapabilities
member.summary: Declares which s a provider can execute natively. All flags default to false except ; providers opt in to the operations their data source genuinely supports.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.SchemaMigration.SchemaMigrationCapabilities.SupportsTransactionalDdl
member.summary: True when the provider can wrap mutating DDL in a transaction.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.SchemaMigration.SchemaMigrationCapabilities.IsReadOnly
member.summary: True when the data source's schema is owned externally and cannot be mutated (SaaS APIs, queues, streams, read-only model files). All mutating ops return with an explicit "unsupported" message.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.SchemaMigration.SchemaMigrationCapabilities.Supports(TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.SchemaMigration.SchemaMigrationOp)
member.summary: Returns true if the provider declares support for the given operation.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.SchemaMigration.ISchemaMigrationProvider
member.summary: Translates logical s into a data source's NATIVE API. Providers NEVER return SQL strings to the caller — they execute directly and return an outcome. A provider is constructed with the owning so it always reflects the live connection.
member.remarks: Resolution is 3-tier (see ): exact DataSourceType override → DatasourceCategory fallback → the null provider. Operations a provider does not support return with a clear message — they never throw and never no-op.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.SchemaMigration.ISchemaMigrationProvider.DataSourceType
member.summary: The data source type this provider serves.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.SchemaMigration.ISchemaMigrationProvider.Category
member.summary: The data source category this provider serves.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.SchemaMigration.ISchemaMigrationProvider.Capabilities
member.summary: What this provider can do natively.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.SchemaMigration.SchemaMigrationProviderAttribute
member.summary: Marks a class as a Tier-1 override for a specific . Mirrors the existing [AddinAttribute] discovery pattern. The class must expose a public constructor taking a single argument (the live data source being migrated); the registry invokes it reflectively.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.SchemaMigration.SchemaMigrationProviderAttribute.DataSourceType
member.summary: The data source type this provider overrides.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.SchemaMigration.SchemaMigrationProviderAttribute.Category
member.summary: The data source category this provider belongs to.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.SchemaMigration.SchemaMigrationResults
member.summary: Builds outcomes for schema-migration providers. Shared so every provider (engine-resident bases and datasource-colocated overrides) returns consistent error shapes. has no dedicated "unsupported" value, so unsupported operations are expressed as with a stable message prefix.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.SchemaMigration.SchemaMigrationResults.UnsupportedPrefix
member.summary: Prefix used for "operation not supported" messages so callers can detect them.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.SchemaMigration.SchemaMigrationResults.Unsupported(System.String,TheTechIdea.Beep.Utilities.DataSourceType)
member.summary: Standard "this provider cannot perform this operation on this data source" outcome. Providers return this for every operation their does not declare as supported.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.ObservableBindingList`1.RejectChanges

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.ObservableBindingList`1.RejectChanges(`0)
member.summary: Reverts a single item's changes. Restores Modified items from snapshot, removes Added items, restores Deleted items.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.FileManager.Attributes.FileReaderAttribute
member.summary: Decorate any IFileFormatReader implementation with this attribute. FileReaderRegistry discovers it at startup via AssemblyHandler — the same pattern as [AddinAttribute] connectors and [PipelinePluginAttribute] pipeline plugins.

example.code: [FileReader(DataSourceType.Parquet, "Parquet File Reader", "parquet")] public class ParquetFileReader : IFileFormatReader { ... }

member.summary: Exposes file-reader discovery and runtime selection behavior for file datasource hosts.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.FileManager.IFileDataSourceReaderHost.GetAvailableReaderDescriptors(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Returns available reader descriptors. When is true, implementations should trigger registry discovery before returning descriptors (if a registry is available). When false, implementations should return cached/discovered data and may fall back to factory-registered readers.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.FileManager.IFileDataSourceReaderHost.GetAvailableReaderTypes
member.summary: Returns all available format types known by the host.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.FileManager.IFileDataSourceReaderHost.GetCurrentReaderDescriptor
member.summary: Returns metadata for the currently active reader selection, or null when no reader is currently selected.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.FileManager.IFileDataSourceReaderHost.GetCurrentReader
member.summary: Returns the currently active reader instance as an opaque object, or null when no reader has been resolved yet.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.FileManager.IFileDataSourceReaderHost.TrySwitchReader(TheTechIdea.Beep.Utilities.DataSourceType,System.Boolean,System.String@)
member.summary: Attempts to switch the active reader to . Implementations should return false with a reason when the switch cannot be completed and should avoid throwing for expected failures. On success, the datasource type should be updated to match the new reader.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.FileManager.IFileDataSourceReaderHost.TrySwitchReader(System.String,System.Boolean,System.String@)
member.summary: Attempts to switch the active reader to a specific implementation type name. This enables selecting between multiple readers for the same datasource type.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.FileManager.IFileDataSourceReaderHost.TrySwitchReader(System.Guid,System.Boolean,System.String@)
member.summary: Attempts to switch the active reader by stable reader identifier.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.FileManager.IFileDataSourceReaderHost.ResetReaderSelection
member.summary: Clears any manual reader override and returns selection behavior to datasource default.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.FileManager.IFileDataSourceReaderHost.TrySwitchReaderByExtension(System.String,System.Boolean,System.String@)
member.summary: Attempts to switch the active reader by file extension (without leading dot).

member.summary: Central repository for all pipeline framework constants. Never use magic numbers or strings in pipeline code — reference these instead.

member.summary: Outcome of a single record validation rule.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Interfaces.ValidationOutcome.Pass
member.summary: Record passed all rules — continue to main sink.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Interfaces.ValidationOutcome.Warn

member.summary: Record failed a rule — route to the error sink.

member.summary: Result of validating a single record. Immutable — created by the validator and consumed by the engine.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Models.DataClassification

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Models.DataClassification.Public

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Models.DataClassification.Internal
member.summary: Internal use only — may appear in logs but not external outputs.

member.summary: Contains business-sensitive data — mask in logs, restrict access.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Models.DataClassification.Restricted
member.summary: Regulated PII / PCI / PHI — mandatory masking and audit trail.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Models.MaskingStrategy

member.summary: Key-value configuration forwarded to the scheduler plugin via Configure().

member.summary: Whether this schedule is active. Disabled schedules are loaded but not started.

member.summary: Maximum simultaneous runs for this pipeline. Default 1.

member.summary: Per-run timeout in seconds. 0 = no timeout.

member.summary: How to retry failed runs.

member.summary: Rate-limit guard for this schedule.

member.summary: Other schedule IDs that must complete successfully before this one fires.

member.summary: UTC time of the next scheduled run (computed by the scheduler plugin).

member.summary: UTC time the schedule last ran.

member.summary: Status string of the most recent run (e.g. "Success", "Failed").

member.summary: UTC time this definition was created.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Models.ScheduleDefinition.ModifiedAtUtc
member.summary: UTC time this definition was last modified.

member.summary: Owner (team or individual) accountable for this schedule.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Models.ScheduleDefinition.Steward
member.summary: Optional steward responsible for data quality of the pipeline output.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Models.ScheduleDefinition.FreshnessSlaSeconds
member.summary: Freshness SLA in seconds — maximum allowed age of data before an alert fires. 0 = no SLA.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Models.ScheduleDefinition.WorkloadClass
member.summary: Workload class used for queue isolation and concurrency quotas. E.g. "critical", "standard", "backfill". Default "standard".

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Models.ScheduleDefinition.RunMode
member.summary: Run mode: "full" (complete refresh) or "incremental" (CDC/watermark-based). Default "full".

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Models.ScheduleDefinition.Watermark
member.summary: Configuration for incremental/CDC runs. Only used when is "incremental".

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Models.ScheduleDefinition.DependencyMaxWaitSeconds
member.summary: Maximum seconds to wait for upstream dependencies before timing out. 0 = wait indefinitely.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Models.ScheduleDefinition.CircuitBreakerThreshold
member.summary: Circuit breaker: consecutive failure count before auto-disabling this schedule. 0 = circuit breaker disabled.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Models.ScheduleDefinition.ConsecutiveFailures
member.summary: Current consecutive failure count (runtime state, persisted for continuity).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Models.ScheduleDefinition.CircuitBreakerTripped
member.summary: When true, this schedule was auto-disabled by the circuit breaker.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Models.ScheduleDefinition.Tags
member.summary: Tags for classification and filtering.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Models.WatermarkConfig.WatermarkColumn
member.summary: Column name in the source entity that holds the watermark value (e.g. "ModifiedDate").

member.summary: Data type of the watermark column: "datetime", "long", "string".

member.summary: Overlap/lookback seconds subtracted from the watermark to guard against late-arriving data. Default 0 (no overlap).

member.summary: Retry policy applied to a scheduled run when it fails. Distinct from PipelineRetryPolicy (which is an exponential-backoff engine helper).

member.summary: Number of additional attempts after the first failure (0 = no retries).

member.summary: Base delay in milliseconds before the first retry.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.IBeepAudit
member.summary: Unified, opt-in audit-trail contract for the Beep stack. Audit events are lossless by policy (never sampled, never silently dropped).
member.remarks: When the audit feature is disabled, is false and every method is a fast no-op (see the null implementation supplied by the engine project).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Models.AuditEvent
member.summary: Phase 08 partial extending with the classification, outcome, change-set, and tamper-evident chain fields. Splitting these into a separate file keeps the original identity surface in AuditEvent.cs and avoids a single monolithic class definition.

Canonical audit-event envelope. This Phase 01 declaration carries only the fields needed for the public surface to compile; the full schema (chain fields, categories, outcomes, field changes) is layered in by Phase 08 via additional partials.
member.remarks: Declared as partial so subsequent phases can add members (notably ChainId, Sequence, PrevHash, Hash, Category, Operation, Outcome, FieldChanges) without forcing a breaking change here.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Models.AuditEvent.Category
member.summary: High-level routing/classification. Defaults to so legacy producers that do not set a category continue to compile.

member.summary: Result of the audited operation.

member.summary: Failure / denial reason. Optional; present only when is not .

member.summary: Per-column change set for CRUD-style events. Producers should emit only the columns that actually changed to keep audit storage bounded.

P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Models.AuditEvent.Sequence

member.summary: Hex-encoded HMAC of the prior event in the chain. Empty for the genesis event of a chain. Set by the signer.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Models.AuditEvent.Hash
member.summary: Hex-encoded HMAC-SHA256 over canonical payload concatenated with . Set by the signer; verified by IntegrityVerifier.

member.summary: Default chain identifier when none is supplied.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Models.AuditFieldChange
member.summary: One per-column delta captured for an representing a Create/Update/Delete operation. Producers should only emit the columns that actually changed (not the whole row) to keep audit storage bounded.
member.remarks: Both and are stored as so the canonical JSON serializer can keep the source type intact (numeric vs string) — this matters for chain hashing because "42" and 42 must not collide.

member.summary: Value after the change. May be null.

member.summary: Convenience constructor.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Models.AuditOutcome
member.summary: Result of the audited operation. Keeping this an enum (rather than a free-form string) lets sinks index outcomes cheaply and lets dashboards group failures without lossy parsing.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Models.AuditOutcome.Success
member.summary: Operation completed successfully.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Models.AuditOutcome.Failure
member.summary: Operation attempted but failed (system error, validation, etc.).

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Models.AuditOutcome.Denied
member.summary: Operation rejected by authorization / policy.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Models.AuditOutcome.Pending
member.summary: Operation accepted but not yet completed (asynchronous flow).

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Models.AuditOutcome.Compensated
member.summary: Operation was rolled back or compensated after the fact.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Models.AuditQuery
member.summary: Phase 10 partial extending with the fluent builder API. Filter values from Phase 01 are still respected; the builder methods simply assign the same backing properties so calls from older clients keep compiling unchanged.
member.remarks: All builder methods return this so callers can chain (new AuditQuery().User("u123").Between(from, to).Take(100)). Engine-specific fields (, , ) live on the partial so the Phase 01 contract surface stays minimal for hosts that prefer the property-bag style.
member.summary: Phase 10 partial that exposes filter-evaluation helpers reused by every storage engine. Centralizing the predicates here keeps the SQLite, file-scan, and in-memory engines consistent and avoids subtle drift between engines as new filter fields are added.
member.remarks: The helpers operate on a single and return true when the event is a match. Engines that can push a filter into the storage layer (SQLite WHERE clauses) still use for any predicate that cannot be expressed natively (typed property values, for example).

member.summary: Configurable policy for duplicate-event handling. Attached to a to control idempotency behavior per consumer.

member.summary: Whether idempotency checking is enabled. Defaults to true for and . Always false for .

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.StreamandEvents.DuplicateEventPolicy.PreferIdempotencyKey
member.summary: Claim key strategy: when true, prefers IdempotencyKey over EventId. Falls back to EventId when IdempotencyKey is null. Default: true.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.StreamandEvents.DuplicateEventPolicy.Window
member.summary: Time-to-live window for claim entries. After this window expires, the same key can be reclaimed (event reprocessed). Default: 24 hours.

member.summary: Action to take when a duplicate is detected. Default: .

member.summary: Singleton default policy (enabled, 24h window, skip action).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.StreamandEvents.DuplicateEventPolicy.Disabled
member.summary: Disabled policy — no dedup checking.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.StreamandEvents.SchemaWireFormat
member.summary:
Confluent Schema Registry wire format helpers.
Layout:
Byte 0: 0x00 (magic byte) Bytes 1–4: schema ID (big-endian int32) Bytes 5+: serialized payload
All major schema registries (Confluent, AWS Glue, Azure Schema Registry) support this framing for Avro payloads; it can be adopted for Protobuf and JSON Schema too.

member.exception: Thrown when the magic byte is absent or less than bytes are available.

member.summary: New schema can be read by the consumer using the latest schema.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.StreamandEvents.SchemaRegistryCompatibilityMode.Forward
member.summary: New schema can be used to read data written with the latest schema.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.StreamandEvents.SchemaRegistryCompatibilityMode.Full
member.summary: Both backward and forward compatible.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.StreamandEvents.SchemaRegistryCompatibilityMode.BackwardTransitive
member.summary: Backward compatible against all prior versions.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.StreamandEvents.SchemaRegistryCompatibilityMode.ForwardTransitive
member.summary: Forward compatible against all prior versions.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.StreamandEvents.SchemaRegistryCompatibilityMode.FullTransitive
member.summary: Fully compatible against all prior versions.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.StreamandEvents.IStreamStorageProvider
member.summary:
Pluggable persistence backend for the Beep streaming engine.
Implementations handle durable storage of messages, topic metadata, consumer offsets, and retention. The engine () delegates all I/O to this interface; real-time dispatch uses System.Threading.Channels internally.
Built-in providers: InMemoryStorageProvider (dev/test), SQLiteStorageProvider (embedded production). Custom providers can back the engine with any durable store.

member.summary: Human-readable name (e.g. "InMemory", "SQLite", "LiteDB").

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.StreamandEvents.ICheckpointStore
member.summary:
Tracks the last successfully processed offset per topic/partition/consumer-group at the application pipeline level.
Partitions are identified by a string partitionKey. Each checkpoint record includes the topic, consumer group, partition key, and a commit timestamp (). Used by StreamConsumerRuntime to record the last successfully processed position so processing can resume after a restart. Commit only after handler success to guarantee at-least-once outcomes.
Do not confuse with IConsumerOffsetStore. IConsumerOffsetStore operates at the broker cluster group protocol level with int partition indices and long offsets — it is used by cluster infrastructure components (ConsumerGroupCoordinator), not by the application pipeline. See Docs/ConsumerPositionContracts.md for the full layering diagram.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.StreamandEvents.ICheckpointStore.CommitAsync(TheTechIdea.Beep.StreamandEvents.CheckpointRecord,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Persists the checkpoint after a handler has completed successfully.

member.summary: Returns the last committed checkpoint for a topic/partition/group triple, or null if none.

member.summary: Removes checkpoint entries older than .

member.summary: Lists all active checkpoints for the given consumer group.

member.summary: Maximum session duration cap.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.StreamandEvents.SubscriptionType
member.summary: Defines the subscription model that controls how messages are delivered to consumers.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.StreamandEvents.SubscriptionType.ConsumerGroup
member.summary: Kafka-style consumer group: partitions assigned to consumers, at-most-one consumer per partition. Default behavior — backward compatible with existing code.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.StreamandEvents.SubscriptionType.Exclusive
member.summary: Exactly one consumer per subscription. A second consumer attempt fails immediately. Use case: ordered processing, singleton workers.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.StreamandEvents.SubscriptionType.Shared
member.summary: Round-robin dispatch across all consumers — no partition affinity. Use case: stateless work distribution, high fan-out.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.StreamandEvents.SubscriptionType.Failover
member.summary: Active-standby: one active consumer, ordered standby list. On active failure, the next standby is promoted. Use case: HA with minimal rebalance.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.StreamandEvents.SubscriptionType.KeyShared
member.summary: Hash-based sticky routing: messages with the same key always go to the same consumer. More flexible than partitions — buckets can be redistributed independently. Use case: per-key processing, session affinity.

member.summary: Owning team/service responsible for governance.

member.summary: Describes how a service subscribes to a topic.

member.summary: Schema versions this consumer supports (for compatibility gating).

member.summary: Duplicate-event handling policy. When null, defaults are derived from : enabled for AtLeastOnce/ExactlyOnce, disabled for AtMostOnce.

member.summary: Subscription type (ConsumerGroup, Exclusive, Shared, Failover, KeyShared). Defaults to ConsumerGroup for backward compatibility.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.StreamandEvents.SchemaCompatibilityMode.None

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Apps.IAppStudioService
member.summary: The App-centric root of the Studio. Everything in the Studio is organised around an : an App owns vironments, each of which owns its s. This is the single App → Environment → Datasource hierarchy the host UI builds on (replaces the old flat environment model).
member.remarks: Every method returns a — business-level failures are reported, never thrown. The implementation wraps the engine's IAppRegistry (persistence) and DatasourceManagementService (live health), so the host never touches those primitives directly.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Apps.IAppStudioService.ListAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: List all apps, ordered by name.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Apps.IAppStudioService.GetAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Get a single app by id or name.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Apps.IAppStudioService.SaveAsync(TheTechIdea.Beep.AppMap.AppDefinition,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Create or update an app. Standard environments are auto-seeded when none are supplied.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Apps.IAppStudioService.RegisterFromSolutionAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Register an app from a solution (.sln). Discovers the solution's projects via the engine's IAppMapService, stamps each with its detected role, and stores the project composition on the app. Data projects become the source of the app's schema.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Apps.IAppStudioService.RegisterProjectAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Register a single project (.csproj) into an existing app. Discovers the project metadata and appends it to the app's Projects list.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Apps.IAppStudioService.ScanAndAddProjectsAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Scan a folder or .sln for projects and add them ALL to an existing app. When a .sln is found, its projects are discovered via IAppMapService and merged (replacing any existing projects with the same name). Web, API, Identity, Service, and Test projects are included — not just Data.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Apps.IAppStudioService.DiscoverEntitiesForProjectAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Discover entity types (data classes) from a project's assembly. Populates AppProject.EntityCount and ModuleNames on the project. Uses the engine's EntityDiscoveryService.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Apps.IAppStudioService.DiscoverAllEntitiesAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Discover entity types for all data projects in the app.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Apps.IAppStudioService.ReScanSolutionAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Re-scan the app's solution path and refresh its project list — adds new, updates existing, removes deleted projects. Use after changing the .sln.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Apps.IAppStudioService.CloneSolutionAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Clone an entire solution (app + envs + datasources + projects) under a new name.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Apps.IAppStudioService.FindNearestSolutionAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Auto-find the nearest .sln file walking up from the given start path. Returns the solution path, or a clean NotFound error when none is found.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Apps.IAppStudioService.DetectChangesAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Detect changes across every environment of an app: schema drift (model vs DB), code versions behind the baseline, and data staleness. Returns one status per env.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Apps.IAppStudioService.ListAvailableDatasourcesAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: List datasources registered in the engine's connection pool (available to bind to any app×env).

member.summary: Generate appsettings.{env}.json for one environment. Bakes every project's endpoint, every datasource's connection string, and the env metadata into a standard .NET config file the host app consumes via IConfiguration. This is the runtime bridge — the Studio is the source of truth for per-env config.

member.summary: Build the composite dashboard view for one app: per-environment cards with live datasource health, plus the promotion status (which envs are behind the baseline).

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Apps.IAppStudioService.PromoteAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Promote the baseline's schema version (and optionally datasource config) to a target environment. Mirrors a Heroku-pipeline / Atlas promote flow: the target's is advanced to the baseline's and its / are stamped. Schema-only. Use for coordinated code+schema+data.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Apps.IAppStudioService.PromoteEnvironmentAsync(System.String,System.String,TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Apps.Workflows.PromoteOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Coordinated environment promotion: advance code (project versions), schema (DB version), and optionally data from the baseline env to the target. This is the full "promote this environment" that ties projects, environments, and the code/schema/data migration tracks together.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Apps.Workflows.IScenarioWorkflow
member.summary: End-to-end scenario workflows for the two primary Studio personas. Each chains the micro-workflows (QuickStart | Migrations | Deploy | Governance | Cicd) into one composite journey so the host UI can offer "Solo Dev" and "Enterprise" as single-decision paths.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Apps.Workflows.IScenarioWorkflow.RunSoloDevAsync(TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Apps.Workflows.SoloDevRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Solo-developer path: create an app with one dev environment, provision several local datasources, bind them to the baseline, migrate each, and optionally seed. Single-developer, no approval gates, local-first by default.

member.summary: Enterprise path: register from a solution, configure multiple environments (dev / test / staging / production), provision datasources per env, set up RBAC + approval gates, deploy code to the baseline, generate a CI/CD pipeline, and record the startup audit.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Contracts.IStudioService
member.summary: Top-level facade for the Beep Studio. The Studio is organised around Apps: an owns environments, each owning datasources. Composes every lower-level service (driver, source, schema, migration, sync, governance, manifest, deployment) behind a single async, platform-agnostic API. UI hosts (Blazor, WinForms, WPF, Maui) call into this; engine primitives (IMigrationManager, BeepSyncManager, IBeepAudit, IDMEEditor) are never touched directly from the host.
member.remarks: This is the **only** type the host UI is expected to depend on. Sub-service facades are exposed as properties so the host can take a single dependency in DI and still reach into any sub-service.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Contracts.IStudioService.Apps
member.summary: The App → Environment → Datasource aggregate. This is the base of the Studio — everything else operates within an App context. Replaces the old flat environment model.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Contracts.IStudioService.Environments
member.summary: Environment tier templates (Dev / Test / Staging / Production) that App environments are stamped from. Drives the approval policy per tier.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Contracts.IStudioService.Drivers
member.summary: Data-source driver provisioning (Phase 2).

member.summary: Source (connection) registry + configuration (Phase 3).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Contracts.IStudioService.Schemas
member.summary: Schema discovery + design (Phase 4).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Contracts.IStudioService.Migrations
member.summary: Migration orchestration (Phase 5).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Contracts.IStudioService.Sync
member.summary: Data sync orchestration (Phase 6).

member.summary: Governance: policies, approvals, audit (Phase 7).

member.summary: Data lifecycle manifest — the link between data and code (Phase 9).

member.summary: Deployment metadata + HMAC approval tokens (Phase 10).

member.summary: Read the Studio's runtime info (version, supported types, capabilities). Synchronous because it is a pure metadata read backed by in-memory state.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Deployment.IDeploymentMetadataService
member.summary: Deployment metadata + HMAC approval tokens. Implemented in Phase 10. The service captures the code revision the Studio is running against, enriches every audit event with it, and issues HMAC-signed approval tokens that bind an approval to a specific code revision.
member.remarks: We do not orchestrate deployments here. The service only resolves the metadata that a CI/CD system has set; it does not build, deploy, push, or publish anything.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Deployment.IDeploymentMetadataService.GetCurrentAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Resolve the deployment metadata for the current process.

member.summary: Governance: policies, approvals, and audit. Implemented in Phase 7. Wraps the engine's existing IBeepAudit pipeline (hash-chained, tamper-evident) with a -shaped surface, an approval workflow, and a redaction policy for sensitive fields.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.IStudioProgress
member.summary: Platform-agnostic progress reporter. The Studio reports lifecycle events (Begin / Update / Complete / Failed) with a structured payload so any UI shell (Blazor, WinForms, WPF, Maui, Console) can render it without knowing engine internals.
member.remarks: This is the cross-cutting contract every Studio host implements. The default implementation is , which silently drops every update (useful for tests and CLI runs).

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.IStudioProgress.Report(TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioProgressUpdate)
member.summary: Emit a progress update. Implementations must be thread-safe.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioProgressUpdate
member.summary: A single progress update from the engine. Carries enough context for the host UI to render a step progress strip, a per-step sub-label, a percentage, a severity (info / warning / error), and an arbitrary payload for operation-specific data (e.g. the current entity being migrated).
member.param: A stable id that correlates all updates for one operation.
The lifecycle stage: Begin at start, repeated Update calls, then Complete or Failed.
A free-form description of the current sub-step (e.g. "Downloading package...").
0..100. The host may clamp to a max for display.
Info / Warning / Error. Failed operations should set this to Error.
When the engine emitted the update.
Operation-specific data (current entity, current row count, etc.).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.Manifest.IDataLifecycleManifestService
member.summary: Data lifecycle manifest — the link between data lifecycle and code lifecycle. Implemented in Phase 9. The manifest is a JSON or YAML file in the project repo that declares: "this code revision expects these data sources, with these schemas, with this approval tier, with this retention policy." The Studio reads it on startup, validates every apply against it, and is the canonical artefact that ties data changes to code revisions.

member.summary: Load the manifest from the configured path. If is null, the Studio walks up from the current working directory looking for beep/data-lifecycle-manifest.json.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioError
member.summary: Error envelope for a . Mirrors RFC 7807 ProblemDetails in spirit but is engine-agnostic. The is a stable, machine-readable identifier the host UI maps to a user-friendly message.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioError.#ctor(TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioErrorCode,System.String,System.Exception,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,System.Object})
member.summary: Error envelope for a . Mirrors RFC 7807 ProblemDetails in spirit but is engine-agnostic. The is a stable, machine-readable identifier the host UI maps to a user-friendly message.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioError.None
member.summary: The default "no error" sentinel used by .

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioError.IsNone
member.summary: True when this is the sentinel.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioErrorCode
member.summary: Stable, machine-readable error codes for . The host UI maps each code to a user-friendly message in a single table; never embed UI strings in engine code.
member.remarks:
The numeric values are stable across releases — do not renumber. New codes are appended at the end. The grouped ranges are:
0-99 — generic
100-199 — driver
200-299 — source / connection
300-399 — schema
400-499 — migration
500-599 — sync
600-699 — manifest + deployment
700-799 — audit
800-899 — cancellation
900-999 — host + internal

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioErrorCode.None
member.summary: No error. The sentinel.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioErrorCode.Unknown
member.summary: An unspecified error. Inspect the for details.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioErrorCode.InvalidArgument
member.summary: One of the arguments did not pass validation.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioErrorCode.NotFound
member.summary: The requested entity does not exist.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioErrorCode.AlreadyExists
member.summary: The entity being created already exists.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioErrorCode.PermissionDenied
member.summary: The caller is not permitted to perform the requested action.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioErrorCode.DriverMissing
member.summary: No data-source driver is registered for the requested type.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioErrorCode.DriverLoadFailed
member.summary: A registered driver failed to load (bad DLL, missing dep, etc.).

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioErrorCode.ConnectionFailed
member.summary: Connection to the data source could not be established.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioErrorCode.ConnectionTestFailed
member.summary: Connection was established but a health check failed (e.g. wrong credentials).

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioErrorCode.SchemaIncompatible
member.summary: The discovered schema is incompatible with what the manifest expects.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioErrorCode.PlanRejected
member.summary: The migration plan was rejected (e.g. by the policy evaluator).

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioErrorCode.PolicyViolation
member.summary: The request violates the manifest's policy (e.g. forbidDestructiveInLive).

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioErrorCode.ApprovalRequired
member.summary: The operation requires an approval token that was not supplied.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioErrorCode.ApprovalTokenInvalid
member.summary: The supplied approval token was rejected (wrong signature, expired, or wrong code revision).

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioErrorCode.ApplyFailed
member.summary: Applying the migration plan failed (one or more DDL ops errored).

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioErrorCode.RollbackFailed
member.summary: Rollback after a failed apply could not complete cleanly.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioErrorCode.SyncRunFailed
member.summary: The sync run was terminated by an error.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioErrorCode.ConflictUnresolved
member.summary: A sync conflict was detected that no rule resolved.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioErrorCode.ManifestInvalid
member.summary: The manifest file could not be parsed or failed validation.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioErrorCode.ManifestVersionUnsupported
member.summary: The manifest's manifestVersion is not supported by this Studio version.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioErrorCode.DeploymentMetadataMissing
member.summary: The current deployment metadata could not be resolved (no git SHA, no env var, no manifest ref).

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioErrorCode.AuditFailed
member.summary: An audit pipeline error prevented the event from being recorded.

member.summary: The call was cancelled via the .

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioMode
member.summary: Operating mode for Beep Studio. Controls which features are visible and whether approval gates are required for promotions.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioMode.Solo
member.summary: One person does everything. No role differentiation needed. All actions available without approval gates.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioMode.Team
member.summary: Multiple developers + DBA + release manager. Role-based views. Approval gates for Staging/Live.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioMode.Enterprise
member.summary: Many teams, many apps, many environments. Full governance, solution control panel, CI/CD endpoints.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioRole
member.summary: User role within Team or Enterprise mode.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioRole.SoloDev
member.summary: Default for Solo mode. Sees everything.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioRole.Developer
member.summary: Writes code, creates schema changes, pushes to Test.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioRole.DBA
member.summary: Manages databases, reviews migrations, approves schema changes.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioRole.Admin
member.summary: Manages environments, promotes to production, governs access.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioModeConfig.EnterpriseAdmin

member.summary: Disable the audit hook. Default: false (audit ON).

member.summary: Disable the built-in hosted services (sync runner etc.). Default: false.

member.summary: Disable the auto-load of plug-in assemblies on startup. Default: false.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioOptions.DefaultEnvironmentTier
member.summary: Default tier applied to environments created via the API when the caller does not specify one. Default: .

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioOptions.AuditRetentionDays
member.summary: How many days of audit history to retain on the local file sink. 0 = forever. Default: 365.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioOptions.ManifestPath
member.summary: Path to the project's DataLifecycleManifest. If null, the Studio walks up from CWD looking for beep/data-lifecycle-manifest.json.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioOptions.EnforceManifestOnApply
member.summary: When true (default), the Studio refuses to apply a migration or run a sync that does not match the manifest's expected source list.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioOptions.EnrichAuditWithDeploymentMetadata
member.summary: When true (default), every audit event is enriched with (code revision + build id).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioOptions.ApprovalHmacKey
member.summary: Override the HMAC key used to sign approval tokens. null means the DI extension will read from BEEP_APPROVAL_HMAC_KEY (or generate an ephemeral key in dev).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Studio.StudioResult`1
member.summary: Result type for all Beep Studio engine calls. Every public mutation or query on returns a instead of throwing for business-level errors. Throwing is reserved for programmer errors (null arguments, contract violations).
member.remarks: The struct is a readonly record struct so it can be returned by value without an allocation on the hot path. The shape mirrors Microsoft.Extensions.Logging-style error envelopes but is engine-agnostic.
